2606.10461.title: ERAlign: Energy-based Representation Alignment of GNNs and LLMs on Text-attributed Graphs
2606.10461.paper_summary: Text-attributed Graphs (TAGs) incorporate textual node attributes with graph structures to describe rich relational semantics. Recent efforts to integrate Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise for learning on TAGs, yet achieving well-aligned representations remains challenging. Prior studies largely rely on heuristics that perform coarse-grained matching. They lack sufficient constraints and ignore distributional alignment, leading to representation drift and limited generalization. Building on Energy-based Models (EBMs), we propose an Energy-based Representation Alignment (ERAlign) framework that projects GNN-encoded graph structure and LLM-derived text embeddings in a shared latent space to achieve distribution consistency. Concretely, layer-wise alignment is quantified by a distance metric and optimized via an EBM objective. By decreasing energy values, our framework yields well-aligned representations for downstream tasks. During training, we introduce Energy Discrepancy (ED) to avoid high sampling costs associated with intractable normalization. ED also carries theoretical guarantees of higher training efficiency and reduced energy landscape distortion. Empirical evaluations on eight TAG datasets demonstrate that ERAlign obtains state-of-the-art performance across varying levels of supervision and cross-task transfer scenarios.
2606.10461.paper_summary_zh: 文本屬性圖（TAGs）結合了文本節點屬性和圖結構，以描述豐富的關係語義。最近將圖神經網絡（GNNs）和大型語言模型（LLMs）整合的努力顯示出在TAGs上學習的潛力，但實現良好對齊的表示仍然具有挑戰性。先前的研究主要依賴於執行粗略匹配的啟發式方法。它們缺乏足夠的約束，並忽略了分佈對齊，導致表示漂移和有限的泛化能力。在基於能量的模型（EBMs）的基礎上，我們提出了一個基於能量的表示對齊（ERAlign）框架，該框架將GNN編碼的圖結構和LLM衍生的文本嵌入投影到共享的潛在空間中，以實現分佈一致性。具體而言，層級對齊通過距離度量進行量化，並通過EBM目標進行優化。通過降低能量值，我們的框架為下游任務產生了良好對齊的表示。在訓練過程中，我們引入了能量差異（ED），以避免與難以處理的正規化相關的高抽樣成本。ED還具有更高訓練效率和減少能量景觀失真理論保證。在八個TAG數據集上的實證評估顯示，ERAlign在不同的監督水平和跨任務轉移場景中獲得了最先進的性能。
2606.10461.author: Xianlin Zeng et.al.
2606.10461.authors: Xianlin Zeng, Fan Xia, Xiangyu Chen
